title: Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
summary: Recursive Language Models (RLMs) apply inference-time compute principles to coding agents for improved performance and reliability. The presentation clarifies distinctions between RLM-based approaches and Claude Code implementations, and demonstrates how both Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse enable recursive task decomposition with declared outcomes.
writeup: Raymond Weitekamp of OpenProse presents Recursive Language Models (RLMs), which apply inference-time compute principles to coding agents to improve performance and reliability. The talk distinguishes RLM-based approaches from Claude Code implementations, and shows how both Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse support recursive task decomposition with declared outcomes. The framing connects test-time compute and recursive composition to practical coding agent design.
